qa.verdict: All 7 acceptance criteria verified. 49 tests pass (40 pre-existing + 9 new). Worker A implementation is correct and complete.
qa.overall_assessment: Worker A correctly implemented _SCRIPT_INTERPS, _is_script_interp(), and _process_script_interp() in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py, eliminating false positives for non-shell script interpreter argv tokens. The dispatch in _process_segment() is correctly ordered (_SHELL_INTERPS first, then _SCRIPT_INTERPS). All 7 AC behaviors verified by direct function calls. No regressions in pre-existing 40 tests. 9 new tests added (exceeds required 7).
qa.user_verbatim_complaint: python3 manage.py rm session-123 triggering the rm-block (false positive in pretool-bash-safety.sh for non-shell script interpreter argv tokens)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: _is_script_interp('r2') returns True because re.split(r'[\d.]', 'r2')[0] == 'r' which is in _SCRIPT_INTERPS (the R statistical language). Commands named 'r2', 'r3' would have argv stripped. Risk is very low as these command names are uncommon in practice.

Path-qualified interpreter /usr/bin/env python3 does not route through _process_script_interp because _find_command_word returns cmd_word='env' (basename of '/usr/bin/env'), which is in _WRAPPER_PREFIXES not _SCRIPT_INTERPS, so the subsequent python3 token is found but already handled differently. The false positive persists for '/usr/bin/env python3 script.py danger-arg'. Pre-existing gap, out of scope for this cycle.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The old generic handler at _process_segment() produced word-kind tokens as-is, causing argv tokens like 'rm' in 'python3 cleanup.py rm /tmp/old' to pass through to the danger-token regex. The new _process_script_interp() strips word-kind tokens containing no shell metacharacters. Verified: strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 cleanup.py rm /tmp/old') now returns 'python3   ' with rm absent from result.split().

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: python3 cleanup.py rm /tmp/old — rm not in word-tokens of output
success_criteria_results.criterion: strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 cleanup.py rm /tmp/old') must not have 'rm' in result.split()
strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 script.py <(rm /tmp/x)') must have 'rm' in result
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct function call; verified tokenization: '<(rm' tokenizes as a single word token containing '<' which is in _SHELL_METAS, so _process_script_interp preserves it
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct call and _tokenize_segment inspection confirming '<(rm' is a word token with shell meta '<'

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: node server.js pkill happy — pkill not in word-tokens
success_criteria_results.criterion: strip_non_executable_contexts('node server.js pkill happy') must not have 'pkill' in result.split()
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct call: strip_non_executable_contexts('node server.js pkill happy') == 'node   '

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: bash -c 'rm /tmp/foo' — rm preserved (shell interp path unchanged)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct function call; bash routes through _process_shell_interp (unchanged), not _process_script_interp. Dispatch order verified: _SHELL_INTERPS check fires first.

success_criteria_results.criterion: python3 -c 'rm /tmp/x' produces rm hidden, consistent with prior behavior
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct function call; verified that _process_script_interp handles kind='single' with out.append('') same as old generic handler
success_criteria_results.details: result = 'python3  ', 'rm' not in result == True. Behavior consistent: old generic handler stripped single-quoted payload; new _process_script_interp also strips it via 'elif kind == single: out.append("")'.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: python3 script.py && rm /tmp/x — rm preserved in second segment
success_criteria_results.criterion: strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 script.py && rm /tmp/x') must have 'rm' in result
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct function call; _split_top_level() splits on '&&'; second segment 'rm /tmp/x' is processed independently as a DANGER_COMMANDS path
success_criteria_results.details: result = 'python3 &&rm /tmp/x', 'rm' in result == True. First segment: python3 (script_interp, argv stripped). Second segment: rm /tmp/x (DANGER_COMMANDS path, unchanged).

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: All 40 pre-existing tests + at least 7 new tests pass
success_criteria_results.verification_method: python3 -m pytest tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v from hooks/ directory
success_criteria_results.details: 49 passed in 0.04s. Pre-existing: TestPass1(4)+TestPass2(4)+TestPass3(4)+TestPass4(6)+TestPass5(1)+TestFailSafeContract(4)+TestDangerCommandArgPreservation(4)+TestBug1CombinedFlagWithC(3)+TestBug2WrapperCommandOptions(3)+TestBug3AbsolutePathShellInPipe(4)+TestDollarVariableRegression(3)=40. New TestScriptInterpArgStripping: 9 tests (exceeds required 7).

build_verification.skip_reason: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py is a Python library imported by a hook, not a deployable web service. Syntax verified by pytest import. No Docker build applicable.

regression_test_results.details: pytest collected 49, all 49 passed. Pre-existing 40 tests verified not broken by new _process_script_interp dispatch path.
python3 -c "x = 1; rm -rf /" -> result 'python3  ""', rm not visible. Old: generic handler stripped double-quote content. New: _process_script_interp applies _strip_double_content. Same result.
python3 -c 'import os; # rm is not needed here' -> result 'python3  ', rm not visible. Consistent with prior behavior.
python3 manage.py $(whoami) -> result 'python3  $(whoami)', subst token preserved by else branch in _process_script_interp. Command substitution in argv remains detectable.

standards_compliance.notes: Hook implementation file — no design-token role table applicable

e2e_enforcement.blocking_reason: No UI or web service involved. This is a Python hook library. Behavior verified by direct Python function calls and pytest.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Hook blocked codex invocation with: BLOCKED: Killing happy/claude/docker processes is forbidden. The QA prompt text contained the words 'pkill' and 'rm' as part of documenting AC test cases, triggering the very false-positive behavior this fix addresses. This is an ironic and self-demonstrating instance of the bug being fixed.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted per graceful fallback contract. 6 adversarial questions tested: (1) r2 false-positive from _is_script_interp (low risk, recorded as out-of-scope observation), (2) dotnet5 versioned name handling (correct), (3) danger argv without -c flag (correct stripping behavior), (4) shell var $HOME in argv (correctly stripped as word-kind with no metas), (5) $(subst) in argv preserved as subst-kind (correct), (6) regression verification of pre-existing test behaviors under new dispatch path (all consistent). No issues threaten the requirement. Verdict: PASS.
